title: pittosporum tonkinense
intro: Pittosporum tonkinense, also known as the Chinese cherry or tonkin tree, is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The seeds of Pittosporum tonkinense have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-viral properties, as well as the potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
